question.text: The IFSCA (AML, CFT and KYC) Guidelines apply to which of the following?

answer.text: Every Regulated Entity under IFSCA and, to a specified extent, the Financial Group of the Regulated Entity
Only banking units within the IFSC
Only insurance companies registered with IFSCA
Only fund management entities registered under IFSCA Regulations

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The provisions of IFSCA (AML, CFT and KYC) Guidelines apply to every Regulated Entity falling under the purview of IFSCA. The provisions also apply to a Financial Group of the Regulated Entity, to a specified extent. A Financial Group consists of a parent company exercising control together with branches and/or subsidiaries subject to AML/CFT policies at group level.
**The Trap:** The Guidelines don't apply only to one type of entity — they cover ALL IFSCA-regulated entities (banks, insurers, fund managers, broker-dealers, etc.) plus their Financial Groups.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Unlike Series XXIV which focuses on SEBI-registered intermediaries, IFSCA-01 covers a unified regulatory framework spanning banking, insurance, capital markets, and fund management within IFSC.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IFSCA is a unified regulator — one set of AML/KYC rules applies to ALL financial activities in IFSC. This is different from mainland India where SEBI, RBI, IRDAI each have separate AML frameworks.
